description: Expose SimOPA as a scorer mode behind the FastAPI recommendation API.
high_score_value: Proves the demo is backed by local model inference rather than mock-only output.
limitation: Subprocess model loading is slower than an in-process persistent scorer.
next_upgrade: Add full/lite scorer modes and runtime comparison.

description: Score OPA labeled placement and generated prior candidates, then rank Top 3.
high_score_value: Turns a single score model into an object-placement recommendation workflow.
limitation: Current evidence covers 100 cases; 6 positive cases remain high-score boundary cases outside Top 3.
next_upgrade: Use boundary cases to discuss SimOPA score saturation and candidate diversity.

description: Compare object mask, coarse bbox mask, and blank mask for the same composite candidates.
high_score_value: Shows that the mask channel materially changes scores and Top 3 membership.
limitation: This is an ablation of the SimOPA input, not a backbone rewrite.

description: Apply temperature scaling and map scores to recommended/acceptable/rejected tiers.
high_score_value: Makes saturated model scores easier to interpret in the Web app and report.
limitation: Calibration is post-hoc and does not retrain the scorer.
next_upgrade: Surface confidence prompts in the Web UI.

description: Remove highly overlapping candidates from ranked results with an IoU threshold.
high_score_value: Reduces duplicate Top 3 recommendations and clarifies boundary cases.
limitation: Does not solve score saturation when multiple distinct candidates score near 1.0.
next_upgrade: Add frontend warnings for saturated or overlapping Top 3 results.

description: Render success, boundary, false-positive-risk, and clear-rejection examples.
high_score_value: Provides concrete artifacts for teammate-owned report/PPT and live demo.
limitation: Only five cases are visualized; not a statistical benchmark.
next_upgrade: Connect built-in Web samples to these representative cases.

description: Run a 6x6 occlusion sensitivity test and render heatmaps for representative cases.
high_score_value: Shows which image regions affect SimOPA scores and supports model-explanation credit.
limitation: Occlusion is slower than a single forward pass and currently offline.
next_upgrade: Link heatmaps from the Web case area.

description: Aggregate end-to-end and per-candidate runtime evidence from existing experiments.
high_score_value: Gives the project a single runtime table for local inference proof.
limitation: Some rows use batch-level logs rather than per-call model timers.
next_upgrade: Use this table to compare full/lite modes after lite mode lands.

area: Web demo workflow and visual polish
description: Load stable demo cases, export recommendation evidence, surface confidence prompts, and switch to a classroom demo layout.
evidence_artifacts: Web UI validation V016/V017; /api/demo/cases; exported JSON/CSV; presentation mode
high_score_value: Makes the app easier to demo, improves scanability, and reduces live-presentation risk.
limitation: Current validation used Playwright fallback because the in-app Browser runtime failed to start.
next_upgrade: Keep future UI work focused on evidence handoff rather than decorative redesign.

description: Compare simopa-full with simopa-lite, a candidate-budget mode that scores fewer placements.
high_score_value: Adds a speed/quality trade-off story without claiming a newly trained lightweight network.
limitation: Lite mode reduces candidate evaluations; it is not a separate trained backbone.
next_upgrade: Optionally train LightOPA ResNet18/MobileNet if a true lightweight network is needed.

description: Perturb mask shape, candidate position, and candidate scale to test score stability.
high_score_value: Adds high-standard reliability evidence without replacing the main model.
limitation: Current evidence is five representative cases, not a large statistical benchmark.
next_upgrade: Optionally repeat on the 50-case split if runtime budget allows.

description: Load SimOPA once in a JSONL worker process and reuse it across candidate-scoring requests.
high_score_value: Cuts 50-case full ranking time from 168.6s to 23.4s while keeping Top 1, Top 3, and assessment identical.
limitation: Worker mode is local-process serving; production concurrency still needs queueing and lifecycle hardening.
next_upgrade: Use simopa-worker for live demo once startup is complete.

description: Train a small 4-channel CNN on an OPA subset using composite RGB plus foreground mask.
high_score_value: Adds a real trained lightweight-model baseline and removes ambiguity between lite serving mode and model-level lightweighting.
limitation: The tiny model is an exploratory baseline with modest accuracy, not a replacement for SimOPA.
next_upgrade: Use C014 residual LightOPA as the stronger lightweight baseline comparison.

description: Train a stronger residual 4-channel CNN on a larger OPA subset and compare it with the tiny LightOPA baseline.
high_score_value: Adds a stronger model-level lightweight baseline: accuracy rises from 0.65 to 0.6717 and ROC-AUC from 0.6761 to 0.7084.
limitation: Residual LightOPA is still an exploratory scorer and has not replaced the production SimOPA worker route.
next_upgrade: Use this result to justify future ResNet18/MobileNet experiments only if more model work is needed.
